title.raw: Teresa M. Scott, DDS, MAIBDM, AIAOMT, Certified Biological Dentist
title.rendered: Teresa M. Scott, DDS, MAIBDM, AIAOMT, Certified Biological Dentist

content.raw:
Teresa M. Scott, DDS, MIABDM, AIAOMT, Certified Biological Dentist Dr. Teresa Scott is an honors graduate of St. Pius X High School, and University of St. Thomas, both here in Houston. Graduating Magna Cum Laude with a Bachelor’s degree in Biology in 1991, she went on to extend her education, receiving her doctorate from University of Texas Health Science Center at San Antonio Dental School in 1995. She received both the Jurisprudence Award, and the Award for Excellence in Clinical Dentistry while still in dental school. She is a member in good standing of the American Academy of General Dentistry, the International Academy of Biological and Dental Medicine (IABDM), the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology (IAOMT),  the Holistic Dental Association (HDA) and the American Academy for Oral Systemic Health (AAOSH). Recognizing that modern conventional dentistry still uses toxic mercury, Dr. Scott has ensured we are a mercury safe office by becoming SMART certified AND ACCREDITED by the IAOMT & a CERTIFIED BIOLOGICAL DENTIST by the IABDM. She has also earned her Mastership AND Fellowship in Biological Dentistry with the IABDM, and is president of the organization from March 2020 through October 2023.  Dr. Scott is an example of absolute and true commitment to the cause of biological dentistry. Dr. Scott has committed to continuing education throughout her career and has steadily worked through the rigorous requirements to advance in her knowledge and skill sets. She regularly takes classes from all of the biological dentistry organizations, as well as additional continuing Dental Education in general dentistry, averaging over 150 hours of CE annually, when state licensure only requires us to take 15 total hours per year.  In 2020, when everyone else took time off during the shutdown, Dr. Scott finished the remaining tasks needed to complete both her fellowship and mastership in Biological dentistry, earning the coveted AIAOMT, FIABDM and MIABDM designations.   She is an award winning dentist:
  • Houston’s Top Dentists, H Texas Magazine, 2007 – Present
  • America’s Top Dentists, Consumer’s Research Council of America, 2005 – Present
Dr. Teresa Scott has built a successful practice by delivering the best in family dental care since 1997. She has been married to her husband Dan, since 1999, and they have a grown daughter, Iliana, whom they adopted from Russia when she was 12 years old, and 2 biological daughters, Grace and Promise, whom Dan homeschools.  She has an incredibly unique story in terms of what she is doing now and why, and it ALL speaks to intention and authenticity in terms of her patient care, as well as her “thinking outside the box” approach to both her own health as well as her patients’. Her journey into biological dentistry started at her youngest daughter Promise’s premature birth in early 2009.  Dr. Scott was already suffering the effects of mercury toxicity and was very sick and unable to breastfeed, but had not yet discovered the root cause of her many health issues.  Promise showed serious allergies to every single commercial formula out there, including the organic and non dairy ones.  Everything that touched her stomach that wasn’t real breast milk caused her to projectile vomit.  And back in those days, it was incredibly hard to find a donor for human milk.  We completely recommend the Human Milk 4 Human babies site for ladies who cannot breastfeed their children or who simply don’t make enough milk, and would like to find someone who is blessed with abundance and is willing to share.  However, even now, it’s not always possible to find donor milk.  When Promise started projectile vomiting everything man made, the pediatrician wanted to put her on the broken down protein formulas and reflux medicines, and Dr. Scott decided that there had to be a better solution out there.  She figured that babies had been born for millenia who did not have a wet nurse available, and modern formulas had only been around for about 75 years at the time. She asked her pediatrician to prescribe milk from the Austin Milk Bank for the short term, until she could do some research and find a better solution.  The Austin Milk Bank provides donated milk for babies, but it is NOT FREE (except for babies covered by Medicaid, believe it or not!).  At the time, it cost $4.25 per OUNCE, and Dr. Scott knew that this was not economically sustainable, as it was not covered by her medical insurance.  However, it bought her 5 weeks to do research and make her plan.  During that time, Dr. Scott found the Weston Price Foundation and found their homemade baby formula recipe.  They connected with a raw milk coop driving group, a website that connects people to providers of Raw Milk.   After she got all of her ingredients for the raw milk formula, she gave it to Promise, despite the pediatrician’s dire warning that the raw milk would cause the baby to have intestinal bleeding, and…. She THRIVED.  THAT was the beginning of Dr. Scott questioning everything she had ever learned about medicine and dentistry.  Because not only did Promise NOT have ANY issues with the raw milk formula, it caused her to grow and thrive into a happy, healthy baby.   For the next 3 years, Dr. Scott learned everything she could in terms of nutrition, essential oils (the family who headed the raw milk coop introduced her to them), and organic living.  However, despite excellent conventional medical care, Dr. Scott continued to get sicker and sicker.  The conventional doctors had no answers.  More importantly, they never questioned WHY she was getting sicker and sicker.  They just kept prescribing more pharmaceuticals.  At one point, Dr. Scott was on 12 different prescription pharmaceuticals, finding no improvement in her health. But her children were getting healthier and healthier as a result of Dr. Scott’s choice to forego conventional medical care for them and seek alternative approaches.  After 3 years of studying and making changes in her personal life, Dr. Scott finally discovered that it was likely mercury toxicity that was making her so sick, and that Dentistry was probably causing it!  She had to make a decision - either to quit doing what she loved, or find a way to marry what she did to what her new holistic paradigm was.  So in 2012, Dr. Scott made the decision to switch entirely to biological dentistry.  She became a member of the biological dentistry organizations, and committed to learn everything she could to both help her patients and reclaim her health.   Unfortunately, Dr. Scott was so sick already from mercury toxicity by that time that the damage was already done in terms of health issues. On December 1, 2015, she received a devastating diagnosis and death sentence.  She was diagnosed with grade 4, stage 4B endometrial cancer and given a year to live, and a 1% chance of survival by her conventional medicine doctors.  Rather than accept her fate, Dr. Scott took everything that she had been learning since 2009, and applied it to her own health.  Though she did accept the hysterectomy and focal radiation therapy for pain control on her hip, where her metastasis was, she refused all other forms of conventional treatment, electing instead to seek alternative methods of healing.  Her reasoning was this:  If conventional medicine could only offer her a 1% chance of survival, she was going to find something that gave her hope for her better odds.  And if she was going to die anyway, she would die on her own terms.  She watched her father and grandmother die from cancer.  She knew what that conventional route looked like.  And so she refused it.  It took her several months to find the providers who could help her with her choices.  Once she started, she was able to achieve remission in 90 days from IV vitamin C and nutrition.  Dr. Scott is quick to point out that everyone is different, and that she can’t claim to know how to help someone else with their cancer journey, but she is a fierce advocate for medical freedom of choice as a result of her experiences.    It’s this kind of outside the box thinking that saved her life, and is now serving her patients as well.  It is also why her patients can see her authenticity and commitment, from our Holistic Dental Associates Social Media Page to her Food for Living page to her public speaking engagements.  he has also become a speaker for Healing Strong, an organization committed to holistic and integrative healing and thriving from cancer diagnoses.  She is truly the REAL DEAL. It is this set of unique circumstances that set Dr. Scott on her current path, and also what makes her different from other health care providers.  She looks for root cause, and then works toward eliminating the oral contributions to systemic health issues.  This is her mission and passion, and she pursues it with enthusiasm and determination.   This is also what makes Dr. Scott so authentic.  Having come back from the brink of death, she is determined to do what she can to help others avoid having to go that far or lose their lives.  She doesn’t pretend to have all of the answers, but she surely has worked incredibly hard to get her life back, and she is committed to helping her patients do the same.   To that end, Dr. Scott not only works full time as an airway centered biological dentist, but teaches airway and biological dentistry to others who want to go down that path. When she is not busy working, Dr. Scott loves anything that involves traveling with her husband and daughters, and is an adrenaline junkie, especially if it involves Jetskis, ATVs or Snowmobiles.  :-)

title.raw: Katie To, DDS, FAGD, DSD, MIABDM
title.rendered: Katie To, DDS, FAGD, DSD, MIABDM

content.raw:
Being a working mom with a young child, I often downplayed my poor sleep habits, foggy brain, and my ongoing fatigue as the norm. That is, until my body went into complete chaos. Once I got well, I became committed to helping others who may have suffered just like me, instead of experiencing confusion and the suffering. They are now experience relief and joy.

Hi, I'm Dr. Katie To, a certified biological and cosmetic dentist from Katy, TX. I've survived mercury toxicity and I'm proud to help others with holistic approach to pain free, non-evasive cosmetic dentistry for a beautiful and healthy smile, body and mind. Our health model is much more enjoyable and long lasting for both patients and the doctor. We listen, we care and go beyond fixing the symptoms. We listen to what is important to you.

You deserve health and happiness. And don’t forget there is always a reason to smile. Don’t hesitate to reach out for any questions. I’m here for you.

title.raw: Charles Cuprill, DMD, IBDM, FIAOMT, MIABDM, AFAAID, FICOI
title.rendered: Charles Cuprill, DMD, IBDM, FIAOMT, MIABDM, AFAAID, FICOI

content.raw:
Dr. Cuprill did his undergraduate studies at George Mason University in Fairfax, Virginia, after finishing up High School with honors in Puerto Rico. He finished with a Bachelor in Science (BS) in Biology. After completing his undergraduate degree, Dr. Cuprill continued his education at the University of Puerto Rico School of Dentistry, where he received his DMD. After deciding to continue honing his skills further, he enrolled in the General Practice Residency (GPR) program at Brookdale University Hospital & Medical Center in Brooklyn, New York. While in his residency, Dr. Cuprill was chosen to be Chief Resident, and decided to stay for a second year of GPR. Dr. Cuprill then graduated with a Fellowship in Implantology & Biomaterials. This title grants him credentials in the American Academy of Implant Dentistry (AAID), of which a Associate Fellow of the American Academy of Implant Dentistry (AFAAID) was bestowed upon Dr. Cuprill. As of 2015, Dr. Cuprill is also a Fellow of the International College of Oral Implantologists (ICOI). Dr. Cuprill has completed board certification in Integrative Biological Dental Medicine and received his board certification in Naturopathic Medicine from the American College of Integrative Medicine and Dentistry (ACIMD). Having completed accreditation credentials, Dr. Cuprill is a Fellow in the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology (IAOMT), where he is currently a board member and the 2nd Vice President of the IAOMT.  He has also recently completed Certification in the International Academy of Biological Dentistry and Medicine (IABDM), and is working on his Fellow status.

The Eco Dentistry Association has certified our practice as the First Gold GreenDOC Dental Practice in Central Florida, making our office one of a kind.

Dr. Cuprill has been in private practice here in Florida for the past 16 years, serving Brevard County in Rockledge and prior to establishing Orlando Holistic Dental Studio, he was working in a dental practice at the RDV Sportsplex in Maitland.

Dr. Cuprill and his wife, Anja, have lived in the Lake Nona/Lake Hart area for the past 16 years. They are very excited to be able to serve neighbors and members of their community for the years to come, helping to further dental health in the community and improve the smiles and oral health of the Lake Nona/Lee Vista area.

Dr. Cuprill loves to play Ice Hockey with good friends, and is an avid Hockey and Football fan. Aside from time with the family, he also loves music, and along with Anja loves to go to concerts and other activities. You can also bump into the Dr. and family at some of the local food trucks.

title.raw: Sheri (Shahrzad) Salartash, DDS, MAGD, IBDM, MIABDM, FIAOMT, FICOI, FAAIP
title.rendered: Sheri (Shahrzad) Salartash, DDS, MAGD, IBDM, MIABDM, FIAOMT, FICOI, FAAIP

content.raw:
Dr. Sheri is an expert Cosmetic & General Dentist sharing a tremendous wealth of knowledge and experience with her patients and staff. She is very passionate about the results of her work and she knew by the age of 10 that she would be a dentist. She has several certifications in areas of functional, biologic, and naturopathic approaches to dentistry. Her office focuses on Comprehensive Dentistry, Aesthetics and Functional/Physiologic and Neuromuscular Orthodontics with a whole-body approach. Her office is certified mercury-safe and she provides services in sleep apnea solutions and TMJ treatment. She graduated Magna Cum Laude from Virginia Commonwealth University in 2009 and is honored to have received the mastership award with Academy of General Dentistry.

She also has fellowship with International Congress of Oral Implantologists and American Academy of Implant Prosthodontics. She is also Certified Biological Dentist by International Academy of Biological Dentistry and Medicine. She is SMART certified for mercury Safe removal and Accredited by International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology.

Dr. Sheri obtained her degree and Board Certification in Integrative Biological Dental Medicine (I.B.D.M.) and Naturopathic Medicine (N.M.D.) at The American College of Integrative Medicine & Dentistry. Dr. Sheri has been trained in Biomimetic dentistry allowing her to expand upon the regular “holistic” type of dental treatment and take it to the next level by reconstructing teeth the way nature intended only using the latest biologically compatible materials.

She has achieved a Diplomate status with American Board of Dental Sleep Medicine for dedication in treatment and advanced education in management of patients with sleep apnea.

She offers the most advanced dental laser system on the market today. The Fotona Lightwalker allows for an array of procedures that were not available just a few years ago for treating dental problems, sleep problems and enhancing cosmetic procedures. The soft tissue system is non-invasive and shorten healing times and there is no need for drills or needles with no bleeding, and minimal or no pain. When looking for non-invasive solutions for gum diseases, cavities, or other oral issues, this is the way to go.

Many different dental procedures can be done involving the medical laser technology, such as removing cavities and overgrown tissue, treating gum diseases, or whitening teeth. Moreover, laser dentistry is ideal for adults and children who are sensitive to needles or have anxiety of dental work due to their past experiences.

title.raw: James P. Wright, DDS, FIABDM, AIAOMT, DASBA
title.rendered: James P. Wright, DDS, FIABDM, AIAOMT, DASBA

content.raw: CERTIFIED, FELLOW, MENTOR First and foremost, I am a Holistic, Mercury Free, Mercury Safe Dentist with over 24 years of experience. As an Accredited Member of IAOMT, I am committed to removing mercury safely and following the academy standards to protect both our patients and my staff. I use the least toxic, most biologically safe, holistic dental materials available today. It is important to me that we change dentistry and integrate body, mind, spirit and mouth when caring for patients. Clinical experience and scientific study both show us that what happens in the mouth, is reflected in the body and what happens in the body is reflected in the mouth. The best outcomes are achieved when we as dentists, understand that practicing dentistry must include the whole person... Body, Mind and Spirit. Also it is important to integrate cutting edge scientific breakthroughs with holistic, herbal and homeopathic remedies. Working together Modern Medicine, along with timed tested holistic treatments, in my experience provide today's BEST Patient Care! I enjoy a reputation for Quality & Perfectionism: My goal as a Biological Dentist is educating and empowering the public and delivering Beautiful, Gentle, Safe Dental Care to my Patients. My staff and I are committed to creating a paradigm shift, from fear to tranquility. Our Dental Med Spa environment smells and feels like a Holistic Day Spa... It is far more pleasant than patients would have ever imagined possible! Creating Beautiful Smiles and hearing about how people's lives have changed with a Beautiful New Smile or the Safe Removal of Mercury or Other Toxic Materials, can be very emotional and touches my heart! My Patients Inspire Me Every Day! I have learned that listening to patients is the key and that working together, we can create much better outcomes. Many patients come to me for Lumineers, which are my personal favorite, as in most cases Lumineers do not require any removal of healthy natural tooth structure. If teeth have been previously cut down or have large amounts of cavities/decay, I may use Traditional Veneers or Crowns to create a Beautiful, Healthy New Smile. If dentures are the only solution, our patients can choose the "All-On-Four" Implant Denture-Bridges, which remain in patients mouths and only come out typically one time per year, when I remove them for professional cleaning. These state of the art dental appliances are giving patients back up to 90% of their natural chewing ability. With old fashioned, traditional dentures, chewing is limited and dentures must be removed at bedtime and stored in a container with water. Originally from Chicago, I now live in Las Vegas with my wonderful wife Debby and together we have created Four Seasons Dental & Med Spa to give patients state of the art technology and a relaxing Spa experience, combined with Exceptional Quality Dental Care. Although I graduated 1988, I am as excited today as I ever was about attending a class and learning about new developments and techniques. Memberships include; International Association of Oral Medicine & Toxicology (IAOMT), International Academy of Biological Dentistry & Medicine (IABDM), Holistic Dental Association, Mercury-Free Dentists. I served on the Southern Nevada Dental peer review committee for years and I participate in the Give Kids A Smile program.

Also it is important to integrate cutting edge scientific breakthroughs with holistic, herbal and homeopathic remedies. Working together Modern Medicine, along with timed tested holistic treatments, in my experience provide today’s BEST Patient Care! I enjoy a reputation for Quality & Perfectionism: My goal as a Biological Dentist is educating and empowering the public and delivering Beautiful, Gentle, Safe Dental Care to my Patients.

My staff and I are committed to creating a paradigm shift, from fear to tranquility. Our Dental Med Spa environment smells and feels like a Holistic Day Spa… It is far more pleasant than patients would have ever imagined possible! Creating Beautiful Smiles and hearing about how people’s lives have changed with a Beautiful New Smile or the Safe Removal of Mercury or Other Toxic Materials, can be very emotional and touches my heart! My Patients Inspire Me Every Day! I have learned that listening to patients is the key and that working together, we can create much better outcomes.

Many patients come to me for Lumineers, which are my personal favorite, as in most cases Lumineers do not require any removal of healthy natural tooth structure. If teeth have been previously cut down or have large amounts of cavities/decay, I may use Traditional Veneers or Crowns to create a Beautiful, Healthy New Smile. If dentures are the only solution, our patients can choose the “All-On-Four” Implant Denture-Bridges, which remain in patients mouths and only come out typically one time per year, when I remove them for professional cleaning. These state of the art dental appliances are giving patients back up to 90% of their natural chewing ability. With old fashioned, traditional dentures, chewing is limited and dentures must be removed at bedtime and stored in a container with water.

Originally from Chicago, I now live in Las Vegas with my wonderful wife Debby and together we have created Four Seasons Dental & Med Spa to give patients state of the art technology and a relaxing Spa experience, combined with Exceptional Quality Dental Care. Although I graduated 1988, I am as excited today as I ever was about attending a class and learning about new developments and techniques. Memberships include; International Association of Oral Medicine & Toxicology (IAOMT), International Academy of Biological Dentistry & Medicine (IABDM), Holistic Dental Association, Mercury-Free Dentists. I served on the Southern Nevada Dental peer review committee for years and I participate in the Give Kids A Smile program.

Biological dentist at Millennium Dental Associates, Dr. Natasha is a native of Scottsdale, Arizona. She completed her undergraduate degree at the University of Arizona in Tucson, and went on to receive her Doctorate of Medicine in Dentistry degree at Oregon Health and Science University in Portland, Oregon.

Dr. Natasha felt that something was missing from the traditional way of practicing dentistry, and wanted to be able to align her personal beliefs and values with her work. She decided to pursue biological dentistry and joined the IABDM—and went on to become a Certified Biological Dentist, including certification in Protect Protocol and ozone therapy.

Dr. Natasha feels aligned with her purpose and is passionate about holistic wellness. Her favorite part about dentistry is building patient relationships and helping her patients heal from the inside out, while restoring confidence back in their smile. She values nutrition, fitness, yoga, spirituality, meditation, and quality sleep.

When she is not in the office, Dr. Natasha likes to dance, do yoga and pilates, lift weights, attend concerts and music festivals, travel to new places, experience other cultures and foods, and spend quality time with her family on the weekends.

content.raw:
Dr. Olivia Hart is the founder of  Virginia Biological Dentistry, where she blends advanced clinical expertise in biological dentistry with a whole-body, approach to dental care. She is recognized for her specialization in immediate ceramic implant placement, using metal-free zirconia implants supported by PRF, laser therapy, and biologically guided surgical protocols.

Dr. Hart trains dentists across the United States in SDS ceramic implants, PRF-supported bone regeneration, and minimally invasive biological oral surgery. Her work is helping accelerate the profession’s shift away from traditional metal implants toward biocompatible, regenerative solutions that support long-term systemic wellness.

Known for her meticulous diagnostic process, compassionate communication, and commitment to safe, integrative dentistry, Dr. Hart provides patients with modern, metal-free restorative options that protect their health far beyond the mouth. Her leadership,and clinical excellence continue to shape the next generation of biological dentists nationwide.

Dr. Hart's qualifications reflect the depth and breadth of her commitment to excellence in biological dentistry. She is Board Certified in Integrative Biological Dental Medicine by the American Board of Integrative Medicine and Dentistry and Board Certified as a Naturopathic Dentist by the American Naturopathic Medical Certification Board. She also is an Accredited Member of the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology (AIAOMT) in recognition of her long-term commitment to and knowledge of biological dentistry in addition to being IAOMT SMART certified to perform safe mercury amalgam removal. For Dr. Hart, biological dentistry goes deeper than fixing individual tooth; it focuses on finding the root cause and supporting each patient’s overall systemic health.

content.raw: Dr. Adrienne Williams is a Houston-area native and clinical partner at Holistic Dental Associates in Spring, Texas, where she practices dentistry through a whole-body, biologic lens. She earned her Bachelor of Science from Xavier University of Louisiana and her Doctorate of Dental Surgery from the UTHealth Houston School of Dentistry, followed by advanced postdoctoral training in General Dentistry. With over two decades of clinical experience, Dr. Williams has served patients in private practice, nonprofit leadership, and academic dentistry. Her approach centers on prevention, biocompatibility, and identifying root causes—helping patients achieve optimal oral health as a foundation for lifelong wellness. Dr. Williams is a Certified Biological Dentist through the International Academy of Biological Dentistry and Medicine (IABDM) and is PROTECT Protocol certified, reflecting her commitment to safe, mercury-conscious and integrative dental care. She is also an Assistant Professor at UTHealth Houston School of Dentistry and has completed advanced training in orthodontics and clear aligner therapy. Outside the practice, Dr. Williams enjoys time with her family, wellness-focused living, and travel.

Dr. Ara Elmajian has practised dentistry in Vancouver, British Columbia since receiving his Bachelor of Science and Doctor of Dental Surgery from the University of Alberta in 1975. Since graduation he has studied and worked extensively in the fields of chronic illness, head, neck and face pain and temporomandibular joint dysfunction. He has also completed postgraduate work in the fields of orthodontics and prosthodontics.
 
Dr. Elmajian received his Certification as a Biological Dentist in October 2010. His practice consists of biological dentistry as well as the management of chronic pain and illness.
 
Dr. Elmajian has been an advocate for detoxification for many years and recommends and guides his patients through proper protocols suited to their needs. He has extensively studied and adopted the powerful field of homotoxicology and uses homeopathic remedies to support a person’s own healing abilities.
 
Dr. Elmajian has also been involved in research in the fields of myofascial and neural therapy. He was one of the first in these fields to use homeopathic remedies along with local anaesthetic for various required injections. He discovered the revolutionary technique of intraoesseous neural therapy (I.O.N.T.). This has proven to be a valuable tool in the diagnosis of jaw cavitation and in numerous other treatment applications.

Dr. Jill Morris established her dental practice, World Class Dentistry in 1987. The practice is family owned and operated with an on-site dental laboratory.. She achieved accreditation with the American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry in 1999, a privilege shared by only 500 dentists worldwide. She is a fellow of the International Academy of Dental Facial Esthetics. She practices holistic and integrative dentistry. Her practice, World Class Dentistry offers zirconia implants, bite reconstruction, safe mercury removal, and orthotropic orthodontics to children and adults.
 
She is a past scientific editor of dental journals and has been a test consultant on dental products for most of the major dental manufacturing companies. Dr. Morris taught at the prestigious LVI dental school post doctorate teaching facility. She is a member of the West Coast Dental Association, Florida Dental Association, American Dental Association, Academy of Laser Dentistry, American Academy of Dental Sleep Medicine, American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry, and International College of Craniomandibular Orthopedics.
 
She has trained internationally in facial plastics with botox and dermal fillers and is one of the few practitioners trained in the use of the facial cannula for dermal fillers which results in less bruising and swelling. She has trained dentists and other health care professionals in live patient care courses in the techniques of cosmetic dentistry, neuromuscular dentistry, full mouth reconstruction, facial and dental lasers, botox and dermal fillers.Dr. Morris trains other health care professionals at her facility in Sarasota.

Dr. Judson B. Wall has been helping patients to feel better for over fifteen years. He is a graduate of the University of Utah and received his Doctor of Dental Surgery from the West Virginia University School of Dentistry. He has an impressive list of accomplishments and credentials, including Accreditation by the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology, a Fellowship with the American Academy of Craniofacial Pain (July 2010), a Fellowship with the Academy of General Dentistry (June 2007) and an Associate Fellowship with the World Clinical Laser Institute (July 2005). He is locally sought after as a lecturer, teaching and training about metal-free dentistry, zirconia implants, TMJ dysfunction and sleep appliance therapy.
 
Dr. Wall has a commitment to excellence and education that is uncommon. He is a member of the Academy of General Dentistry, American Academy of Craniofacial Pain, International Association of Oral and Medical Toxicology (IAOMT), Holistic Dental Association, and International Association for Orthodontics. He has received advanced training from the Institute of Advanced TMJ Studies, one of the world’s most prestigious TMJ dysfunction therapy education programs, along with Rondeau Seminars, and an ongoing host of Continuing Education courses. He is in the founders circle for the Lumix2 Cold Pulsing Laser, and is among the first dentists along the Wasatch Front to employ this technology.
 
Serving the community is a priority for Dr. Wall and his team. Along with educating elementary students across Davis county about dental health, Dr. Wall is a provider for the â€œGive Kids a Smileâ€ program. While serving in the United States Air Force, Dr. Wall was awarded the Air Force Achievement Medal for Meritorious Service. Other community service recognitions include Salt Lake District Award of Merit for Boy Scouts of America (2006), Chamorro District Scoutmaster of the Year (2004), completion of BSA Woodbadge training (2005), and the Silver Beaver award (2010).
 
In his spare time, Dr. Wall enjoys spoiling his wife, Marisa. Dr. Wall loves teaching his four boys, Keaton, Chase, Brooks and Landon to work hard and play hard. And he is developing his own practice management software on a Mac. He is an avid fly-fisherman, outdoorsman, and especially enjoys water – and snow – skiing. Gardening has become one of his greatest ways to relax. His latest venture is designing a “green” office, including a garden area where patients may walk, eat, relax and heal. The garden will be the source of the fresh vegetable/fruit smoothie of the day.
 
Dr. Wall is also a peer reviewer for professional publications of AGD articles.

People, generally, are in a quandary as to what path to take, root canal therapy for their infected tooth or extraction, traditional tooth cutting fixed bridgework to replace a missing tooth, removable plates for missing teeth, or implant placement for a missing tooth/teeth. Most people, dental doctors included, are unaware of the simple, safe, effective, durable, and efficient alternative of the Carlson Bridge ® "Winged Pontic" replacement system. Our system uses space age composites of exceptionally high strengths, durability and beauty for the construction of the "prefabricated pontic" (space replacing prefab tooth). All your dentist has to do is contour the "prefab pontic" to fit the space where your missing tooth is located, surface prepare the teeth that will hold the pontic and the pontic itself with certain attachment adhesives, adhere the pontic to those teeth with the fusing blue light, and spend a short amount of time artistically shaping, shading, and contouring and characterizing your new bridge. This is done in one appointment at the fraction of the cost of other procedures. We encourage pioneering people to bring this to the attention of their dental doctors. We encourage you to do this gently and humbly since egos are involved and often by one's eagerness to share something with the doctor, for whom the doctor may be unaware, egos get bruised and occasionally fractured. content.raw: Stephen Kovar, DMD Dr. Stephen Kovar has been part of the My Dentist family since 2008. Dr. Kovar completed his undergraduate work in biomedical sciences at Montana State University in Bozeman, Montana. Following MSU, he was accepted and attended Oregon Health Sciences University School of Dentistry where he graduated with honors in 1995. Following dental school, he completed a general practice hospital dental residency at the Wadsworth/UCLA VA Medical Center in Los Angeles. Dr. Kovar also earned his fellowship in the Academy of General Dentistry – a designation that only 5% of dentists in North America receive. After completing his education, Dr. Kovar returned to Portland Oregon where he practiced at Kaiser Permanente and was also a Professional Dental Director for their North Interstate operations where he oversaw a team of clinical staff including general dentists, an oral surgeon, prosthodontist, periodontists, hygienists as well as all other clinical staff. Eventually, he parted with Kaiser Permanente and had a private practice in a Portland suburb and became an Assistant Professor of Restorative Dentistry at Oregon Health Sciences University where he taught both clinically and pre-clinically. Eventually, the rainy weather got the best of him and he relocated to the Phoenix metropolitan area where he has practiced since. Biologically dentistry became a focus for Dr. Kovar who strives to provide the best and safest dental treatment for his patients. Dr. Kovar consistently takes continuing education in excess of the state’s requirements and his educational continuing education includes a great amount of biological dentistry as well as staying current on other dental treatments available. His strive to do more included his achieving status as a “certified biological dentist” by the International Academy of Biological Dentistry and Medicine. When not practicing, Dr. Kovar values time with family and friends. He loves to sing horribly along with the radio, he is a HUGE Disneyland geek, and he loves travel – both domestic and international. Life is short and this world is big! When opportunities to experience life present themselves, it is important to not let those opportunities get away!

content.raw:
William Twohig, DDS

Our goal when we see our patients at Twohig Dental in Weyauwega is to understand their needs from head to toe, not just from the chin to the nose, from a natural and biological viewpoint. We want you to be involved in your own health care, and together we will make the best decisions for you. A dentist must first get to know a person, and then he or she can help them. We want to form relationships for a lifetime.   My name is Dr. William J. Twohig and I have been practicing dentistry for over 30 years. I dedicate my free time to searching for alternative methods of dentistry to offer my patients quality and care for their individual needs. In recent years, more and more individuals are assuming greater responsibility for their own health. There is a dawning awareness that good health is more than the absence of disease. In this regard, I have assumed a primary obligation to provide information and guidance to those people who are seeking to participate in their own health care, and to help in the continuing education of practitioners who have a desire to expand their knowledge and awareness.

Dr. Marilyn Jones received her BS in Biology-Chemistry. She also received her teacher's certificate and began teaching chemistry at Sam Rayburn High School in Pasadena, Texas. Dr. Jones then received her MS in Chemistry from the University of Houston and started a PhD program in Science Education and supervised student teachers. She became an assistant professor of Chemistry at San Jacinto University and then went on to become an assistant professor of Chemistry at the University of Houston Downtown Campus. She taught a six weeks summer institute for physics teachers under the National Science Foundation Institute. Dr. Jones also presented a paper to the Academy of Science on â€œUnobtrusive Measures for High School Students as an Indicator of Interest.â€ This was the topic of her research.
Upon her acceptance to the University of Texas Health Science Center Dental School, she resigned from teaching at the University of Houston.

Dr. Marilyn Jones' heart lies in the health field. Her mother was a nurse and she instilled many health values in Dr. Jones.

title.raw: Al A. Fallah, DDS, MICCMO, CIABDM, AIAOMT
title.rendered: Al A. Fallah, DDS, MICCMO, CIABDM, AIAOMT

content.raw: Dr. Al Fallah integrates a holistic, innovative approach into his state-of-the-art dentistry practice, and specializes in changing the lives of his patients through the skillful, appearance-enhancing cosmetic rejuvenation of their smiles. After receiving a Bachelor of Science degree cum laude in biochemistry from the University of California, San Diego (UCSD), Dr. Fallah graduated in 1987 from UCLA Dental School (University of California, Los Angeles), where he scored in the top 6 percent of the nation on his National Board Examinations. Dr. Fallah is also a graduate of the acclaimed residency program in Contemporary Cosmetic Dentistry in Los Angeles. At the Las Vegas Institute for Advanced Dental Studies, Dr. Fallah learned professional cosmetic procedures from leading authorities. He is certified in using the Invisalign system of invisible braces for orthodontic treatment. Dr. Fallah's progressive education includes the latest techniques in every form of contemporary cosmetic dentistry, including Full-Mouth Reconstruction, treatments for neuromuscular occlusion, jaw orthopedics and temporomandibular joint dysfunction (known as TMJ, or craniomandibular dysfunction). In 2002, he completed a prestigious fellowship at the International College of Craniomandibular Orthopedics. In merging his knowledge of science and the principles of nutrition with his practice, Dr. Fallah works in connection with a network of physicians and other healthcare professionals as a pioneer in the field of chirodontics. This groundbreaking form of treatment includes a whole-body orientation and Craniosacral techniques, and Dr. Fallah's patients often report that they receive great benefits from chirodontics related to relief from chronic head and neck pain.

content.raw:
David Biles, DDS

Dr. David Biles practices biological biomimetic bioesthetic dentistry in Santa Cruz, CA. A dentist for nearly 30 years, Dr. Biles earned his master's degree in exercise physiology from UC Davis 6 months before receiving his DDS from the UCLA School of Dentistry while working nights and weekends at Northridge Hospital as a cardiopulmonary technologist in the ICU, CCU, ER, and NICU during the late 70s. He taught exercise physiology to pre-med, pre-dental, and upper division university students in a NASA and Air Force Academy supported human performance laboratory at UC Davis prior to his stint in medicine. His experiences in medicine, dentistry, and human research have given him a perspective on dentistry that few have.

content.raw: Chester V. Clark, Jr., DDS Dr. Chester V. Clark Jr is dedicated to serving the local community. After 12 years on the city council he currently serves as mayor of Amity and on the Clark County Community Foundation. He is also President of Ouachita Ministries, Inc. which operates a Christian high school and Bible college near Amity. Dr. Clark resides in Amity with Harriet, his wife of 40 plus years. Their four children are now grown and they have 3 grandchildren. One of Dr. Clark's nearly life-long passions is flying. He has been a pilot since 1968 and is the treasurer of a flying club in Hot Springs. The club owns a Cessna 182 which frequently helps Dr. Clark reduce travel time to meetings and events. Other hobbies Dr. Clark enjoys are gardening, spending time with his family, young people, and using his John Deere tractor or bulldozer.

Dr. Francis received his Doctor of Dental Surgery degree from the University of Texas School of Dentistry at Houston. He also holds a Bachelor’s of Science in nutrition from the University of Texas at Austin. He is member of the Academy of General Dentistry, the International Academy of Oral and Medical Toxicology, and the American Dental Association.

Before dentistry, Dr. Francis worked for four years in a holistic family medical practice alongside medical doctors, chiropractors, acupuncturists, and massage therapists. There, he saw firsthand the profound impact a balanced combination of conventional and holistic medicine can have on a patient’s life and well-being. He believes deeply that, given the right building blocks, the body has the ability to heal itself.

Shortly after he began dental school, Dr. Francis met Dr. Glaros at a school vendor fair. It didn’t take long for them to figure out their mutual interest in overall health and biological dentistry, and Dr. Glaros soon became his trusted mentor.

Dr. Francis is excited to now be working in this caring environment where he spent so many days shadowing Dr. Glaros and learning from him – a place where patient health and well-being always come first.

content.raw:
Zina Aaron DMD 

Being a dentist allows me to help people smile with confidence, restore function and comfort, and relieve often intense pain. My goal with each patient is to establish a long-term, trust-based relationship. I want people to feel comfortable with me and my team, and confident in my care recommendations. I always have the patient’s best interests at heart and care for them as I would my own family. I was a frequent dental patient in my youth and have experienced many dental procedures firsthand. I can sympathize and empathize with my patients’ feelings and anxiety. I strive to make sure each patient is fully informed, comfortable with their care plan, and encouraged to take control of their oral health. I believe in compassionate care and forming a partnership with each patient, working together to improve the health of their smile. — Dr. Zina Aaron Education and Continuing Education Undergraduate degree – Emory University in Atlanta Doctorate of Dental Medicine – Medical College of Georgia in Augusta One-year general practice residency – Miami VA Medical Center Dr. Aaron seeks to help her patients achieve long-term dental and systemic health. By pursuing continuing education courses, she ensures that she provides the most current treatment and delivers the best results. Her most recent coursework has focused on dental sleep medicine, cosmetic dentistry, and Invisalign®. Zina Aaron DMD 

Being a dentist allows me to help people smile with confidence, restore function and comfort, and relieve often intense pain. My goal with each patient is to establish a long-term, trust-based relationship. I want people to feel comfortable with me and my team, and confident in my care recommendations. I always have the patient’s best interests at heart and care for them as I would my own family.

I was a frequent dental patient in my youth and have experienced many dental procedures firsthand. I can sympathize and empathize with my patients’ feelings and anxiety. I strive to make sure each patient is fully informed, comfortable with their care plan, and encouraged to take control of their oral health.

I believe in compassionate care and forming a partnership with each patient, working together to improve the health of their smile. — Dr. Zina Aaron

Dr. Krithika Jayaprakash received her Doctor of Dental Surgery (DDS)from University of Southern California. She also received her Bachelor for Dental Surgery(BDS) in India.

Dr. Ho was born in Vietnam and grew up in Baton Rouge, Louisiana. She received her Bachelor Degree in Biology from Texas A&M University. Immediately upon graduation, she entered Creighton University School of Dentistry and graduated in the top 2% of her class. While at Creighton University School of Dentistry, she was selected as a member of the national dental honor society, Omicron Kappa Upsilon. The membership was limited to those dental students who have distinguished themselves by excellence in scholarship, while demonstrating exemplary traits of character and potential qualities for future professional growth and attainments. Dr. Ho then got accepted to a class size of two residents at the University of Texas School of Dentistry- Houston to receive three extra years of endodontic specialty training and a Master Degree in Science of Dentistry. During her time as an endodontic resident, she was involved in her own research of root canal irrigation and served as a teaching assistant to dental students. After years of practicing endodontics, she decided to establish her own practice, Cypress Endodontics. Dr. Ho’s philosophy is that a patient can’t see what you’re doing but they can feel what you’re doing and how you’re doing it. For every patient, she strives to hear from them at the end of the procedure that they felt comfortable and confident with her care. To her, that’s professional satisfaction and pride that she always aim for. In her free time, she very much enjoys cooking, spending time with her family and volunteer in the community.
 
Dr. Ho is board eligible and is a specialist member of the American Association of Endodontists and the Houston Academy of Endodontists. She is also a member of the American Dental Association, the Greater Houston Dental Society and the Texas Dental Association. She is fluent in both English and Vietnamese.

Dr. Shetty recognizes there is a fundamental connection between oral health and whole-body health and has fully embraced a biological approach to dentistry. In addition to a number of professional organizations, Dr. Supriya K Shetty is a member of the Huggins Alliance, the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology (IAOMT), the International Academy of Biological Dentistry and the International Association for Orthodontics.

When she is not busy working, she enjoys cooking, listening to music, spending time with her family and traveling. She is fluent in several languages, enjoys a regular meditation practice is interested in Ayurveda and Vedic astrology.

content.raw: Born and raised in Okayama, Japan, Dr. Yuko moved to Pasadena, TX when she was sponsored by the Pasadena Rotary Club as a high school exchange student. She decided to continue her education in the U.S. and subsequently attended Texas A&M University where she earned her B.S. in Biomedical Sciences. After living in Texas for six years, Dr. Yuko decided to attend Boston University Goldman School of Dental Medicine. Becoming a dentist was a dream of hers since she was six years old when she had an unforgettable experience at her dentist who inspired her to become a dentist just like him. Immediately after she graduated from dental school in 2001, she was recruited by one of the most respected professors at Boston University to become a full-time associate dentist at Chestnut Hill Dental where she built her foundation as a caring and compassionate dentist for over fourteen years. In 2014, Dr. Yuko and her husband moved to Chicago for a year. In Chicago, she met Dr. Janet Stopka, who taught her about the dangers of mercury use in dentistry and also introduced her to the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology (IAOMT). After learning how she could offer her patients a safer approach through biocompatible dentistry, Dr. Yuko became a member of IAOMT. Dr. Yuko worked alongside Dr. Stopka for one year and this work led Dr. Yuko to become a proud Accredited Member of IAOMT. Dr. Yuko is one of two accredited IAOMT member in the Boston area (Dr. Mayr is the other one). Dr. Yuko is passionate about improving patients overall health through safer and kinder approaches to dentistry. When she's not caring for her patients, Dr. Yuko enjoys cooking, yoga, meditation, outdoor activities, travel and trail running. She has completed many marathon races including the Honolulu Marathon. Professional Experience: -Former Clinical Instructor at Boston University Goldman School of Dental Medicine – 2015-2019 -Onsite Dental Care (mobile dental service to assisted living communities), – 2015-2016 -Dr. James Schulte, Associate Dentist – 2014-2015 -Chestnut Hill Dental, Full-time Associate Dentist – 2001-2014 Professional Affiliations and Additional Experience: -Member of Academy of Biomimetic Dentistry -Biomimetic Dentist: Levels I, II and III trained -Accredited Member of IAOMT (International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology • https://iaomt.org/) -S.M.A.R.T. Certified (International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology -Member IAMSD (International Association of Mercury Safe Dentists) -Member of SPEAR Study Club -Member of Oral Surgery South Study Club -Volunteered at CommunityHealth (a leader in comprehensive care for low-income, uninsured adults) -Completed Live Implant Course -Certified Invisalign® Provider since 2007 -Associate Fellowship from World Clinical Laser Institute -Certified Yoga Teacher -Certified CrossFit Level One Trainer Education: -Boston University Goldman School of Dental Medicine, Boston, MA – Doctor of Dental Medicine, May 2001 Cum Laude (graduated with honor) -American Academy of Periodontology Award, 2001 -Texas A&M University, College Station, TX – Bachelor of Science in Biomedical Sciences, May 1997

title.rendered: Candida Castillo, DMD, CAGS, FAGD, CIABDM

content.raw:
Dr. Candida Castillo graduated from The University of Rhode Island with a BS in dental hygiene. She pursued her professional goals at Boston University School of Dental Medicine where she earned her DMD and a CAGS for her residency . She was awarded the Dr. Cummings Award for her dedication and unique love for dentistry. During her residency, she was awarded with the outstanding clinical proficiency.

Dr. Castillo is a gifted clinician; her interdisciplinary approach to dentistry is founded in her clinical experience and in her continuing education from world renown educators and leaders such as the Dawson Center and Spear Education. Through the years, she has traveled to dental meetings and conferences seeking out continuums in both general holistic and cosmetic dentistry. Through these numerous hours of continuing education she has built on her foundation of creating healthy, beautiful smiles. She has learned the most current techniques for all aspects of holistic dentistry, providing safe amalgam removal and a linking the body-mouth-connection.

Dr. Candida Castillo is a FELLOW of the Academy of General Dentistry. An honor bestowed upon only 9% of dentists in the country. In addition, Dr. Castillo is a member of the American Academy of Cosmetic Dentistry and the American Dental Association.

Dr. Castillo creates awareness of the importance of good oral health by volunteering in the community. She volunteers at the Providence Rescue Mission, RI Mission of Mercy, New England Lupus Foundation, and other health fairs throughout the year. Dr. Castillo has been voted by her peers "RI TOP DENTISTS", in RI montly Magazine since 2015.

She has a unique practice philosophy that patients recognize the second they walk through the door. Whether looking for general, family, or cosmetic dentistry in Rhode Island, Dr. Castillo looks forward to giving you an awesome experience.

content.raw:
enlightenDENTAL is a dental practice that believes that we are all connected. The health of the teeth, the gums and the airway are fundamental in the function of a whole body. We utilize biocompatible dental materials for each person in order to reduce the inflammatory response and promote healing. We believe that the health of the mouth and oral biome impact gut health significantly. We utilize technology such as a Fotona laser, PRF, ozone therapy, low dose radiation digital X-ray systems, minimally invasive restorative procedures that mimic nature. We are a mercury safe and fluoride free dental practice. Air quality is of utmost importance. We have a system of surgical grade air filtration and purification units throughout the dental practice to protect our patients and ourselves from airborne pathogens and particulates floating in our breathing space.

SERVICES OFFERED:

  • Alternative materials for dental restorations
  • Biocompatible materials testing
  • Fluoride-free office
  • Mercury-free office
  • Nutritional counseling
  • Oral appliance therapy (for sleep)
  • Airway-focused expansive orthodontics
  • Oral microbiome testing
  • Ozone therapy
  • SMART (Safe Mercury Amalgam Removal Technique)
  • Zirconia ceramic implants
  • Biological surgery with laser, PRF, ozone, etc.
  • Biomimetic dentistry
  • Guided Biofilm Therapy cleanings
  • Low-dose x rays
  • CBCT scans
  • Metal free denture options

content.raw: Gorelik Dentistry   Dr. Julia Gorelik is dedicated to helping you achieve your goals for your smile so you can smile with confidence, knowing that your smile represents you perfectly.   Dr. Gorelik graduated from NYU College of Dentistry in 2001; before attending NYU, she was a dentist in Russia and graduated from St. Petersburg Medical University in 1995. While she has been practicing dentistry for several years, Dr. Gorelik is dedicated to consistently furthering her knowledge in the dental field by regularly participating in continuing education courses, seminars, and conferences. To stay current with the latest in state-of-the-art dentistry, she is a member of a number of professional organizations and study clubs, including:   -American Association of Cosmetic Dentistry -North Carolina Dental Society -Charlotte Dental Association -Charlotte Implant Study Club -International Academy of Oral Medicine & Toxicology -International Congress of Oral Implantologists   In addition, Dr. Gorelik has received advanced training in implant dentistry to ensure that each patient receives the highest possible level of care. She is also SMART-certified through the International Academy of Oral Medicine and Toxicology, meaning she is trained in their Safe Mercury Amalgam Removal Technique. Throughout her career, she has displayed a relentless passion for excellence in periodontal health, function, and dento-facial aesthetics. She also believes in a team approach to achieving optimal results for her patients, collaborating with both dental and medical specialists that she has personally selected for their skill and integrity. When you visit Gorelik Dentistry, you can rest assured that you are truly in the best of hands.   Dr. Gorelik is a second-generation dentist – her mother practiced as a dentist for 40 years. Dr. Gorelik is proud to carry on her tradition of personalized dentistry and excellent patient care.   When she isn’t working to enhance your smile, Dr. Gorelik enjoys reading, traveling, concerts, theater, museums, exercising, and spending time with her family, which includes her 13-year-old daughter.

content.raw: Sorriso Dental    Dr. Caroline Labritz grew up in dentistry with her father, Dr. P. Ronald Graziani, who maintained a successful practice in Fairmont since 1976. Dr. Labritz spent many summers and holiday breaks helping in the office which sparked her interest in following in her father’s footsteps.     Dr. Labritz graduated from the West Virginia University School of Dentistry in 2004 with a Doctorate of Dental Surgery Degree. She spent her first six years practicing in Morgantown until coming back to Fairmont in 2010 to practice with her father in the Graziani Labritz Dentistry practice.     As the years went on, Dr. Labritz felt a desire to return to Morgantown to once again provide services to patients in the area. Her passion and an opportunity came together, and in 2016, she joined forces with Dr. Diana Frum to form Dental Arts. After much thought, Dr. Labritz renamed Dental Arts to Sorriso Dental, a name that represents the practice for not only where it is going, but from where it came from. To pay respect to its previous dentists, Dr. P. Ronald Graziani and Dr. Diana Frum, both of whom have significant Italian heritage, the name Sorriso Dental was chosen. Sorriso means “Smile” in Italian.     Dr. Labritz is an avid learner and researcher of all things dental. With a passion for the profession, she invests a great deal of time on Continuing Education hours in order to provide the highest quality treatments for her patients. Dr. Labritz is a Fellow of the International Dental Implant Association, member of the American Association of Implant Dentistry, member of the Academy of General Dentistry, Monongalia Dental Association, West Virginia Dental Association, and American Dental Association. With additional training in dental implants, modern Root Canal Therapy, Invisalign, Cosmetic Makeovers, you will find her very knowledgeable in all aspects of dentistry. Patients quickly see her gentle manner and special interest in children. Dr. Labritz has a special place for children and prides herself on calming the fears often associated with dental treatment.     Dr. Labritz has spent many years studying advanced dentistry techniques and you can expect an experienced and learned dentist. She has completed training with many of the top leaders in dentistry including Dr. Arun Garg of Implant Seminars, Spear Institute, Clinical Mastery with Dr. John Nosti, Brighterways Institute with Dr. Joe Mehranfar, and the Pikos Institute with Dr. Michael Pitkos. She is committed to always provide a high standard of care.     In her personal life, Dr. Labritz is married with two active boys. She enjoys baking and cooking in her free time. She also likes to read a good book or two. She is very active in fitness and nutrition as well.

Holistic Family Dentist. Special Needs Patient Advocate.
Special interests include sleep health, airway management, early interventional growth & development therapy, & collaborating with Pediatricians & ENT Physicians.
 
My patients call me Dr. Jill, so feel free to do the same. You can read about my credentials below, but I thought it to be most important to share our practice philosophy and how it came to be:
 
We aim to partner with our patients through honest conversations and with the most advanced systems to deliver conservative, comprehensive care in a comfortable and fun environment.
 
Now, this isn’t some random jargon I think sounds good. I really believe in this. And here’s why. While in college, I played Division I field hockey. I loved being an athlete, and working hard and pushing myself were woven into my very being. Unfortunately, I had to stop playing after two years because I started having unexplained episodes of unconsciousness, shortness of breath, head and chest pain.
 
Starting late in my college career, my parents and I went on an exhaustive search that included various specialists in Houston and Dallas to diagnose exactly what the cause was of my symptoms. From neurologists who thought I might have epilepsy or a neurodegenerative disorder, to endocrinologists and immunologists who included autoimmune disorders, food allergies, and hormone issues as part of my differential diagnosis. I think my initial differential diagnosis included about 10 possible systemic conditions, and my parents and I saw no less than 20 different specialists for consults and second opinions. It was terrifying, exhausting, and at times, seeminly hopeless.
 
During my second year of dental school, we finally found a team of cardiologists who properly diagnosed my condition as having a worsening PFO, or patent foramen ovale. It is a hole in the heart that exists in all babies in the womb, but once born, it closes for proper functioning of the heart. Sometimes the hole doesn’t close properly, and that is a cause of some pediatric heart surgery cases. Very rarely does someone grow to my age without having this condition discovered. Shortly after the diagnosis was made and verified, I was scheduled for heart surgery, and I went on to have two open heart surgeries over the course of 11 months to repair the defect in my heart.
 
After recovering from the surgeries, I continued my training in dental school and graduated with my Doctorate of Dental Surgery only 16 months after my second surgery. I’m also very proud to say that I went on to complete a half-marathon within a year of my second surgery, proving to myself that I was still that athlete that could work hard and achieve new goals.
 
And while I would never wish to go through any of this, I know that this journey made me the compassionate healthcare provider I am today. I know the struggle of going to multiple providers to figure out what’s going and how confusing it can be when you have multiple “professionals” all telling you different things. I know that awesome feeling of restored health. And I know that in the big picture when things can be stressful, a sense of humor and a little fun can really make a difference.
 
And it is this “empathy + honesty + exceptional care + bad jokes” burrito of a dental office philosophy that I hope to bring to each one of my patients. I am genuinely interested in understanding what your priorities are and how I can help you achieve your goals.

Dr. Minh Luu graduated from the University of California, Riverside with a degree in Biochemistry. She then moved to Boston to attend Tufts University, School of Dental Medicine where she completed her dental education and was an active member of various community service organizations.

Dr. Luu has since achieved numerous awards for exceptional patient care as an associate dentist in her previous practices. She has also appeared on FOX CT News twice to educate the public about the importance of dental health and prevention, how dental health affects overall systemic health, and to promote free dental care days for the under-served population and for Veterans.

Being born and raised in California, Dr. Luu has had the privilege of experiencing the culture of the east coast, having lived in Boston and Connecticut for a combined 8 years before transitioning to Houston. She loves spending time with family, cooking, traveling, beaches, and being active outdoors (except for the hot Houston summer days).

Dr. Luu understands the struggles of visiting the dentist, such as fear, financial roadblocks, and time. These hurdles can prevent anyone from achieving optimal oral health and esthetics. This is precisely why she believes in making great dentistry a reality by building a trusting relationship with patients through empathy and individualized treatment plans.

Dr. Gandhi has been working in the dental field since 2006, and her passion for helping others smile brighter has only gotten stronger over time. She has lived in Murphy for several years now and feels very proud to serve the needs of this wonderful community.

Dr. Gandhi chose to become a dentist because she loved the way the field combines art and science, as well as the presence of a healthy work/life balance. She has a natural drive to help others, and oral healthcare has been the perfect fit for her! Today, she can’t imagine doing anything else.

Dr. Gandhi’s path started in India, where she completed her dental degree in 2006 and worked as a general dentist while pursuing a career in oral diagnosis and pathology. She then moved to the United States, settling in Murphy in 2009 and working as a dental assistant. In 2011, she began the Advanced Education program for international dentists at the Oklahoma College of Dentistry, moving back to Murphy after graduation. For her, the learning never ends, so she still takes continuing education classes at Baylor College of Dentistry and is an active member of Spear Education. She is also certified in Invisalign and nitrous oxide.

I have been part of the dental community near Cincinnati for over 25 years, and a lifelong Ohioan. My journey into dentistry began at Miami University in Oxford, OH, where I earned a Bachelor’s degree in microbiology. My Doctor of Dental Surgery degree was granted by The Ohio State University College of Dentistry in 1998.  From there I completed a General Practice Residency at the Medical College of Ohio.

I have long been a strong advocate of preventive dentistry and educating patients about the connection between the mouth to the rest of the body. I know that avoiding problems in the mouth saves the patient time, money, and discomfort, and promotes overall good health. I also work closely with the finest specialists in the area, to ensure healthy, attractive results for every patient I serve.

I served as President of our county dental society and am a current member of the American College of Lifestyle Medicine.

Living a healthy, happy and clean lifestyle is a lifelong passion of mine and I hope to improve the lives of my patients by educating them about what I have learned over the years.

Dr. Sara Gholam is a board-certified pediatric dentist and the founder of Magnolia Pediatric Dentistry. Her practice is mom-owned, wellness-minded, and based in Lancaster County, Pennsylvania. She offers prevention-first, relationship-based care in a calm, Montessori-inspired setting designed to honor each child as an individual and support whole-body health.

In her early career, Dr. Sara worked as a contractor with the Environmental Protection Agency, where she gained firsthand insight into how environmental toxins can impact long-term health.

That experience shaped her passion for prevention-focused dentistry — a path she continued at Penn Dental, where she was selected for the prestigious Community Oral Health Honors Program dedicated to public health and outreach.

At Magnolia, care is collaborative — with fluoride-free options, biocompatible materials, and a strong emphasis on parental partnership. Dr. Sara’s goal is to create a space where families feel truly seen, supported, and empowered in their child’s oral health journey.

Holistic Dental Wellness of Los Angeles
The founder of the Holistic Dental Wellness of Los Angeles, Dr Emma Abramyan, is a recognized biological doctor with nearly two decades of experience as a clinician, researcher, and educator. Dr. Emma Abramyan is a Holistic SMART Certified dentist, one of a few fully Certified Biological dentists in Southern California. Her industry knowledge includes ozone therapy, biological laser surgery, minimally invasive biomimetic dentistry, sleep and airway health, early pre-orthodontic treatment for kids, anti-aging regenerative treatments, and clinical nutrition. Within each area of expertise, biocompatible and nontoxic approaches are selected, safe patient care being a top priority. Our team specializes in Biological dentistry and wellness, placing equal importance on proper nutrition, posture, sleep, airway, mindset, and emotional wellness. Dr Emma personalizes all dental materials, techniques, and procedures to maximize optimal wellness for each patient. As a board-certified laser surgeon, Dr Emma uses cutting-edge technology to improve oral and systemic health, the intricate bidirectional mouth-body connection, as she connects with each patient and they share their journey with her, she listens carefully and offers insights to improve all aspects of health: Mind, Body and Spirit. They then co-create an individualized treatment plan that supports their long-term well-being.
EMPOWERING PATIENTS & PEERS WITH A FRESH PARADIGM
Helping to transform a patient’s health and empowering each patient to enjoy wellness is at the core of Dr Emma’s philosophy. With every patient, she brings a mindful, soothing, and calm disposition to her work and their dialogue. She knowledgeably explains each step of the treatment and utilizes gentle biological techniques to perform state-of-the-art procedures in the scope of Holistic dentistry. Dr Emma never stops learning. Most recently she completed vigorous training in Sleep and Airway Health, along with Natural Anti-Aging Regenerative Treatment modalities for Biological Beauty and Wellness. With more than 1,500 hours of personal CE completed, Dr Emma surpasses the standards for the State of California by approximately 12 times the annual requirement. Targeting most of her CE studies with Holistic dentistry and wellness, she remains at the forefront of her fields. A Frequent speaker on the topics of oral-systemic link and longevity, Dr Emma also travels the world with a passion for educating the public about biological mindset. Through an endless pursuit of knowledge, Dr. Emma and her team at the Holistic Dental Wellness of Los Angeles are equipped to walk alongside and support their community with modern and innovative techniques that inspire Holistic wellness. Dr Emma Abramyan is listed in the Guide to America’s Top Dentists, Continental Who’s Who Amongst Professionals, and Top Holistic Dentist of Los Angeles.

content.raw: Laguna Hills Center for Sleep Apnea & CPAP Intolerance Dr. Angham (Ann) Abdulla DDS grew up in Kentucky before her family decided to move to San Diego, California, where she attended high school. Having developed an interest in the medical field, she decided to study psychology at the University of California, Irvine, and earned her bachelor’s degree in 1988. Deciding to pursue a dental career, she attended Loma Linda University School of Dentistry to earn a Doctor of Dental Surgery degree. Loma Linda University provides advanced training in oral healthcare based on a comprehensive, holistic approach to dentistry. While there, she was an assistant professor of Restorative Dentistry and participated in “Clinic with a Heart,” an LLU program that provides free dental care to those in need. Because of her positive experiences with “Clinic with a Heart,” Dr. Abdulla traveled to Mexico and Thailand after graduating to continue her volunteer work. Since then, Dr. Abdulla has become one of a few Orange County dentists with extensive experience and specialized training in the treatment of sleep apnea and TMJ pain. As an American Board of Dental Sleep Medicine (ABDSM) Diplomate, she is qualified to use oral appliance therapy to treat obstructive sleep apnea. Additionally, having completed a TMJ and Orofacial Pain Mini-Residency at UCLA, she is an expert in using holistic, non-surgical methods to effectively treat TMJ pain disorders. To stay up to date on the latest advances in dental sleep medicine and oral appliance therapy, Dr. Abdulla regularly attends American Academy of Dental Sleep Medicine (AADSM) annual meetings. As a certified SMART dentist, she is qualified to perform safe amalgam filling removal, and offers non-toxic, mercury-free composite fillings as a biocompatible alternative. In addition to her academic credentials, advanced training, and more than 25 years of experience, Dr. Abdulla is also a member of: - American Dental Association - California Dental Association - Orange County Dental Association - International Association of Oral Medicine and Toxicology - American Academy of Dental Sleep Medicine - 1-800-Snoring (where she is a founding member) Contact the Laguna Hills Center for Sleep Apnea & CPAP Intolerance today to learn more or to schedule a consultation.

Dr. Aryani earned her dental degree from the New York University College of Dentistry in 2003. She serves honorably in the USAF as a Captain in Dental Corp, right after graduating. Throughout her 20 year career, she has kept up-to-date with more than a 1000 hours of continuing education classes to ensure her patients receive the most comprehensive & advanced care. She is also a graduate of the prestigious Dawson Academy and is a fellow of AAOSH (American Academy of Oral Systemic Health)and a member of IABDM (International Academy of Biological Dentistry and Medicine).

Dr. Aryani practices the concept of “holistic,  biologic dentistry and functional orthodontic”,  which focuses on getting to the root cause of patient care – emphasizing prevention & early intervention. This practice philosophy requires understanding that there is a connection between systemic disease and oral conditions. Did you know, for example, that 50% of all heart attacks are caused by periodontal pathogens and that diseases such as diabetes, high blood pressure, heart attacks, ADHD, depression, obesity and sleep apnea can have their roots traced to airway issues, tongue position and the development of the upper and lower jaws. So addressing the oral systemic link could save you from many systemic problems in the future. Dr. Aryani identifies these issues before any symptoms appear, so you can get and remain healthy.

Our Dental Practice’s Philosophy

We understand that your oral health is a mirror to your overall wellbeing. We specialize in zirconia implants, sleep apnea solutions with NightLase® therapy, and holistic approaches to orthodontics, cosmetic dentistry and teeth whitening. Our practice is grounded in the belief that preventive care and conservative treatments are key to maintaining long-term health. We strive to provide care that is not aggressive but instead focuses on preserving as much of the natural tooth structure as possible.

Experience and Expertise

Dr. Baker, who began practicing dentistry in 2012, brings a wealth of experience in general dentistry to our office, allowing him to have a deep understanding of how holistic dentistry can benefit patients. We are committed to modernizing dental care by integrating traditional dentistry with holistic health principles.

Family-Oriented Dental Care

Dr. Bender is an active member of the American Academy of Cosmetic Dentists, American Dental Association, Oklahoma Dental Association, American Women’s Dental Association, and Oklahoma County Dental Society.
 
Through the Give-Back-A-Smile Program, Dr. Bender volunteers her time to helping restore the smiles of domestic abuse victims, re-establishing their confidence and health as they rebuild their lives. Dr. Kelly J. Blodgett is Oregon’s only Certified Biological Dentist both by the IABDM and the International Institute of Biological Dentistry.  He is one of the world’s most recognized experts in the field of biological dentistry and holistic oral health. His career has been marked with a bevy of national and international awards. He in not only an exceptionally gifted clinician, but also a gifted communicator.  He is leading the profession of dentistry to an approach that focuses on the humanistic emotions and feelings that accompany healthcare experiences.

His upcoming book, “Feel Whole Again,” is revolutionizing the approach to oral health care and redefining the modern dental experience.  His approach starts with humanizing dental visits by dealing first and foremost with the toxic emotions that people carry from their past dental experiences. He uses the goals, values, timeline and budget of each unique human being he treats as the guideposts rather than traditional dental dogma. Dr. Blodgett’s mantra of “First psychology, then biology optimized by technology” has helped thousands of human beings reclaim not only their physical health, but their mental and emotional balance, as well.  Prepare yourself for a time of self-discovery and health optimization as you realize the power of the humanistic modern dental experience at Blodgett Dental Care!

content.raw: Stacy Cole, DDS Born in Abilene, Texas Dr. Cole spent his elementary school years in the west Texas town of Snyder. His family moved to Fort Worth, Texas when he was in the sixth grade. Other than college at Baylor University in Waco, National Guard active duty in Fort Knox, Kentucky and Baylor College of Dentistry in Dallas, he has lived in Fort Worth ever since. Dr. Cole's wife and partner since 1975, Pat B., also a Baylor graduate, works in the office as a massage therapist, seeing patients who need relief from head and neck pain. She is trained in craniosacral techniques as well as many other modalities. The Coles have had the great joy to see their two children, Audrey and Southworth, grow up into extraordinary young adults. They feel blessed by their presence in Fort Worth and even more so with four dear grandchildren. Dr. and Mrs. Cole attend Christ Chapel Bible Church in Fort Worth where they are active in a community group and Bible studies. Dr. Cole promotes a culture of continual improvement, both professionally and personally. He is passionate about reading, studying and attending workshops to continually learn more about natural healthcare and new technologies. Dr. Cole is active in the holistic healthcare scene and frequently speaks to groups in the community about dental awareness from the holistic perspective. Dr. Cole practices what he preaches! His favorite hobby is training with kettle bells to stay fit, and as often as possible run on the Trinity River trails that meander throughout Fort Worth. His favorite leisure pastime is watching Baylor Bear football from the home game sidelines as team dentist, and providing care for Baylor University athletes from various sports. Dr. Cole genuinely enjoys life! Professionally, he is a member of the American Dental Association and Texas Dental Associations and he is active in the Fort Worth District Dental Society. He is a Fellow of the Academy of General Dentistry, a Fellow and board member of the American Academy of Craniofacial Pain, a Diplomat of the American Academy of Pain Management and he has been Board Certified as a Naturopathic Physician. Dr. Cole has over 3,500 credit hours of continuing education and continues to learn, teach, write articles and pursue innovative ways to address difficult cases with optimum esthetics and health.
